## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

heartbeat

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

1	## Context
2	
3	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
4	
5	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
6	
7	## Single operator prerequisite chain
8	
9	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
10	
11	## Entry contract — load-bearing
12	
13	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
14	
15	- Working tree clean
16	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
17	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
18	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
19	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
20	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
21	
22	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
23	
24	## Failure mode contract
25	
26	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
27	
28	## Release-branch execution contract
29	
30	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
31	
32	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
33	
34	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
35	
36	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
37	
38	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
39	
40	## Tier layering
41	
42	Three tiers, escalating cost:
43	
44	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
45	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
46	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
47	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
48	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
49	
50	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
51	
52	## Operator scope
53	
54	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
55	
56	## Coupling to ₣A_
57	
58	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
59	
60	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
61	
62	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
63	
64	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
65	
66	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
67	
68	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
69	
70	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
71	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
72	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
73	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
74	
75	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
76	
77	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
78	
79	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
80	
81	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
82	
83	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
84	
85	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
86	
87	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
88	
89	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
90	
91	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
92	
93	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
94	
95	## References
96	
97	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
98	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
99	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
100	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

143	# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
144	# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
145	# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
146	# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
147	# Lifecycle is determined first via CRM v1; only ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED
148	# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files
149	# named "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" (state) and
150	# "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" (project_id) are written via
151	# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
152	# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
153	#
154	# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
155	# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
156	#
157	# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
158	# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
159	#
160	# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
161	#
162	# Args: <oauth_access_token>
163	zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
164	  zrbgp_sentinel

# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
# Lifecycle is determined first via CRM v1; only ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED
# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" (state) and
# "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}" (project_id) are written via
# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
#
# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

  local -r z_billing_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
  local z_billing_page=1
  local z_billing_page_token=""
  local z_billing_url=""
  local z_billing_tok_enc=""
  local z_billing_infix=""
  local z_project_count=0
  local z_index=0
  local z_global_project_index=0
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_crm_infix=""
  local z_crm_code=""
  local z_lifecycle_state=""
  local z_state=""
  local z_sa_url_base=""
  local z_sa_url=""
  local z_sa_page=1
  local z_sa_page_token=""
  local z_sa_tok_enc=""
  local z_sa_infix=""
  local z_sa_list_code=""
  local z_sa_count=0
  local z_sa_index=0
  local z_sa_email=""
  local z_mason_email=""
  local z_mason_local=""
  local z_moniker=""

  while :; do
    z_billing_url="${z_billing_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_billing_page_token}"; then
      z_billing_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_billing_page_token}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode Billing v1 pageToken"
      z_billing_url="${z_billing_url}?pageToken=${z_billing_tok_enc}"
    fi

    z_billing_infix="depot_state_billing_${z_billing_page}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_billing_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "${z_billing_infix}"

    z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
      || z_project_count=0

    z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1)); continue; }

      # CRM v1 lifecycle lookup gates the SA list — cheaper call, single
      # classification decision before potentially-failing IAM scan.
      z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_global_project_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" \
        "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
        "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true

zrbgg_roster_role() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local -r z_role="$1"
  local -r z_fact_ext="$2"

  buc_step "Rostering ${z_role} service accounts in project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"

  local z_url_base="${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}"
  local z_page_token=""
  local z_page=1
  local z_count=0

  while :; do
    local z_url="${z_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
      local z_tok_enc
      z_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode pageToken"
      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
    fi

    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER}_${z_role}_${z_page}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts (page ${z_page})" "${z_infix}"

    local z_sa_count
    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
      || buc_die "Failed to parse service accounts page"

    local z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
      local z_sa_email
      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".accounts[${z_index}].email") \
        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${z_role}-(.+)@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}$ ]]; then
        local z_identity="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        buf_write_fact_multi "${z_identity}" "${z_fact_ext}" "${z_sa_email}"
        buc_bare "  ${z_identity}  ${z_sa_email}"
        z_count=$((z_count + 1))
      fi
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
    done

    z_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.nextPageToken') || z_page_token=""
    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
    z_page=$((z_page + 1))
  done

  buc_info "Found ${z_count} ${z_role} service account(s)"
  buc_success "Roster operation completed"
}

rbgg_roster_retrievers() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Roster Retriever service accounts (emit per-identity fact files)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zrbgg_roster_role "${RBCC_role_retriever}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever}"
}

rbgg_roster_directors() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Roster Director service accounts (emit per-identity fact files)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zrbgg_roster_role "${RBCC_role_director}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director}"
}

rbgg_invest_retriever() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Retriever service account for an identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_role_retriever}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_retriever}-${z_identity}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Investing Retriever service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                                          \
    "${z_account_name}"                                                        \
    "Recipe Bottle Retriever (${z_identity})"                                  \
    "Read-only access to Google Artifact Registry - identity: ${z_identity}"   \
    "${RBCC_role_retriever}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Retriever SA"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Artifact Registry Reader role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Artifact Registry Reader"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "retriever-reader"

  buc_step 'Adding Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                              \
    "${z_token}"                                         \
    "Grant Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"                           \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"                  \
    "retriever-analysis"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Install:"
  buc_bare "        cp ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
}

#

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Source RBBC bootstrap constants (source-time, before kindle)
test -n "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_CONFIG_DIR not set - rbcc requires launcher environment"
source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
RBCC_role_governor="governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
RBCC_role_payor="payor"
RBCC_role_assay="assay"
RBCC_role_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Cult-verb tinder — used by SA-management surface (invest/divest/roster) and
# composed into fact-extension constants below.
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_director}"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set - rbcc requires BURC environment"

  # Kindle constants (depend on runtime state)
  readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.text:
## Recommendations

### 1. Total-collapse vs additive
**Recommend: total collapse.** Delete the standalone `depot-lifecycle` and `governor-lifecycle` cases. The fused flow's depot stand-up + tear-down absorbs case 2's exercise; case 3's mantle is absorbed by the SA cycle. **One nuance to preserve:** case 2's post-unmake `DELETE_REQUESTED` tolerance assertion (current rbtdrp_pristine.rs:678–685) is a specific behavioral check against the soft-delete graveyard — fold it into the tear-down case rather than dropping it silently.
**Confidence: High** that rev [6] prescribes total collapse. **Medium** on the post-unmake assertion preservation.

### 2. Section name
**Recommend: `pristine-lifecycle-cycle`.** Function-named (matches `-gate` and `-simple` style), and avoids the slight misnomer of `-credentialed` (the section also does uncredentialed depot stand-up + tear-down). Fallback `pristine-lifecycle-credentialed` if naming continuity with the pace silks "phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed" matters more than precision.
**Confidence: Low-Medium.** Cosmetic; trivially renameable.

### 3. Fused-flow case decomposition
**Recommend three cases, in order:**

- `depot-stand-up`: install throwaway prefixes (idempotent helper) → list-pre → pick moniker (family `pristl`, single family across both roles since one depot serves both) → install moniker → levy → list-present → read project_id from `<moniker>.depot-project` fact → tee for diagnostics. No stdout-grep assertions.
- `sa-cycle`: mantle (rbw-aM) → tee `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` → invest retriever (identity `pristl-ret`) → `std::fs::copy` assay→canonical retriever → access-probe retriever → invest director (identity `pristl-dir`) → copy assay→canonical director → access-probe director → divest director → divest retriever → rm assay file. Static identities (depot is throwaway-per-run, so collision-free).
- `depot-tear-down`: unmake (BURE_CONFIRM=skip) → list-after → assert project_id absent or `DELETE_REQUESTED`.

Governor cleanup folded into unmake per rev [6]; no separate forfeit governor step.
**Confidence: Medium-High** on shape (constrained by docket). **Medium** on identity strings — judgment call.

### 4. Manifest required colophons
**Recommend final set:**
```
DEPOT_LEVY, DEPOT_LIST, DEPOT_UNMAKE,
GOV_MANTLE,
GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER, GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR,
GOV_DIVEST_RETRIEVER, GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR,
ACCESS_PROBE
```

Add four new colophon consts (`rbw-arI`, `rbw-adI`, `rbw-arD`, `rbw-adD`). Remove `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_LIST_SAS` and `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_FORFEIT` from the manifest entirely after verifying no other fixture references them (a quick grep confirms — they were pristine-only). Roster colophons not required: the fused flow doesn't need to discover SAs by listing.
**Confidence: High.** Mechanical given BBAAN's landed surface and rev [6]'s explicit instruction.

### 5. Stdout-substring retirement
**Recommend:** Replace all stdout-grep assertions with file-system primitives:
- Project_id: read from `<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}` fact via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact` after the post-levy list invocation (one fact-read, as docket specifies). Don't use `rbtdrp_compose_project_id` for the assertion path — that's still a Rust-side derivation that doesn't prove the depot was actually created. Reserve compose for diagnostic tee.
- SA presence: rely on access-probe success as the assertion (a probe pass proves the SA exists with correct roles). No list-grep.
- SA-email tee: keep as diagnostic-only via `rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email` read post-mantle; no assertion against stdout.
- Post-unmake: walk fact files, not stdout. After the post-unmake list, `<moniker>.depot` fact's content should be `DELETE_REQUESTED` or absent — assert via file-content check.

**Confidence: High** on retirement strategy. **Medium** on the post-unmake mechanism (file-content check vs absence check, depending on whether `rbgp_depot_list` re-emits facts for soft-deleted depots).

### 6. RBRA assay→canonical movement
**Recommend: let assay overwrite naturally between roles.** Sequence: invest retriever → assay-file deposited → `std::fs::copy(assay, canonical_retriever)` → access-probe → invest director → assay-file overwritten with director's key → `std::fs::copy(assay, canonical_director)` → access-probe → both divests delete production RBRAs. As the SA-cycle case's last step, `std::fs::remove_file(assay)` to leave a clean post-condition for the marshal-zero gate at next-run-start. Don't introduce per-role assay subdirs or rotation — the operator flow doesn't, and rev [6]'s "mirror operator-flow file movements" is the constraint.
**Confidence: Medium-High.** Mechanism is straightforward; the only judgment is the explicit final rm — could equally be left for marshal-zero to catch, but explicit is cleaner.

---

## Summary of confidence

| # | Topic | Confidence |
|---|-------|-----------|
| 1 | Total collapse | **High** (collapse) / **Medium** (preserve DELETE_REQUESTED check) |
| 2 | Section name | **Low-Medium** |
| 3 | Case decomposition | **Medium-High** (shape) / **Medium** (identities) |
| 4 | Manifest colophons | **High** |
| 5 | Stdout retirement | **High** (strategy) / **Medium** (post-unmake mechanism) |
| 6 | RBRA movement | **Medium-High** |

Want me to validate any of the Medium-confidence calls before we lock the design? Specifically: whether `rbgp_depot_list` re-emits a fact for a soft-deleted depot (which determines #5's post-unmake mechanism), and whether `rbgg_*_invest` fatals or overwrites if assay-file exists (which determines whether the explicit cleanup in #6 is just hygiene or load-bearing).

Top recommendation: **`pristine-lifecycle-arc`**

Reads as the rise-apex-fall trajectory: depot stand-up rises, SA cycle is apex, tear-down falls. Short, kebab-fits the existing `-gate` / `-simple` style, and "arc" is doing real work — it names the *shape* of what's distinctive about this section (the three cases form one continuous trajectory through a single throwaway depot, not three independent probes).

Two alternatives if `-arc` doesn't land:

- **`pristine-lifecycle-circuit`** — emphasizes the closed-loop property (start clean, end clean). Slightly longer; also faintly suggests "electrical circuit" which is a wrong-domain ghost.
- **`pristine-lifecycle-throwaway`** — names the depot's disposability. Most descriptive of the engineering character but reads more as an engineering note than a section identity.

`-arc` for the pick.

input.prompt:
You are designing three new test case implementations for the pristine-lifecycle fixture in the rbm_alpha_recipemuster project. Working directory: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.

**Your output is a TEXT DELIVERABLE — do NOT edit any files.** The main thread will integrate your output.

## Context

The pristine-lifecycle test fixture is being rebuilt for pace ₢BBAAK. The current file `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs` has:

- §1 `pristine-lifecycle-gate` — case `rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation` (KEEP unchanged)
- §2 `pristine-lifecycle-simple` — cases `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle` and `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle` (DELETE — being replaced by your new §2)

The new §2 will be `pristine-lifecycle-arc` — a three-case fused section sharing one throwaway depot. The cases run sequentially in the fixture; cross-case state flows through `rbrr.env` (the moniker installed by case 1 persists; cases 2 and 3 re-derive project_id from it via the existing helper `rbtdrp_compose_project_id`).

## Read these files for context

1. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrp_pristine.rs` — full file. Note: helpers `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes`, `rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker`, `rbtdrp_compose_project_id`, `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker`, `rbtdrp_invoke_logged`, `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields`, `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` are all already in place. Static const `RBTDRP_DELETE_REQUESTED = "DELETE_REQUESTED"`. Const `RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY` / `RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP` for unmake env.

2. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — `rbtdri_invoke_global`, `rbtdri_invoke_imprint`, `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`, `rbtdri_Context`, `rbtdri_InvokeResult`. Note: `result.burv_output` plus the `RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR` constant locate per-invocation fact files.

3. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` (lines around 2847) — see `rbtdrc_access_probe_role(ctx, role, dir)` for the access-probe pattern. You'll re-use the same shape (or call it directly if reachable; check whether the function is `pub(crate)` — if private, replicate the call inline).

4. `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh` (lines 528-590) — `rbgg_invest_retriever` and `rbgg_invest_director` take an identity arg (param1 channel), write the new SA's RBRA file to `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`. Confirm via reading these files.

5. `Tools/rbk/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh` (lines 74-78) — `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` are all `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/<role>/rbra.env`.

6. `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — `RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"` (the fact-extension for the project_id fact emitted by `rbgp_depot_list`).

## Design decisions (already made — do not redesign)

- **Section name:** `pristine-lifecycle-arc`
- **Family stem for moniker autodetect:** `pristl` (constant `RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC`, value `"pristl"`)
- **Static identities:** retriever `pristl-ret`, director `pristl-dir` (consts `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER`, `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR`)
- **Cross-case state mechanism:** `rbrr.env` (moniker survives across cases); each case re-derives project_id via `rbtdrp_compose_project_id(root, moniker)` where moniker is read from rbrr.env via `rbtdrp_read_env_value(&rbrr, RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
- **No stdout-grep assertions.** Use `Path::exists()` on canonical RBRA paths after invest/probe/divest, and a fact-content read for project_id post-levy. Access-probe success IS the SA-presence assertion.
- **Invest writes RBRA to assay; test copies assay → role canonical.** Static `std::fs::copy` (not rename — preserve assay for diagnostic post-mortem until divest).
- **No separate forfeit governor step.** Tear-down's unmake absorbs governor cleanup per BBAAN's 404-tolerant ordering.
- **Read project_id from fact file** (not stdout, not compose). After `rbgp_depot_list`, read `<moniker>.depot-project` from the BURV output dir via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact_multi` (or whatever the multi-fact reader is named — check rbtdri_invocation.rs for the API). If only single-fact reader exists, read the file directly using `result.burv_output.join(RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR).join(format!("{}.{}", moniker, RBTDRP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT))`.

## The three cases

### Case 2 (NEW): `rbtdrp_depot_stand_up`
Pre-condition: rbrr.env may be marshal-zero blank.

Steps:
1. `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(&root)` (idempotent self-commit).
2. List-pre via `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST, &[], &[], dir, "list-pre")`. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
3. Pick moniker via `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker(&list_pre, RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC)`.
4. Install moniker via `rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker(&root, &moniker)` (self-commit).
5. Levy via `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY, &[], &[], dir, "levy")`. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
6. Re-list to refresh facts: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST, &[], &[], dir, "list-present")`.
7. Read project_id from `<moniker>.depot-project` fact file via direct file read (compose path from `list_present.burv_output`). Tee to `dir.join("project-id.txt")`.
8. Sanity-cross-check: the composed project_id (`rbtdrp_compose_project_id`) must equal the fact-read project_id. If not, Verdict::Fail with diagnostic showing both. (This catches RBDC kindle drift from payor's actual creation.)
9. Verdict::Pass.

### Case 3 (NEW): `rbtdrp_sa_cycle`
Pre-condition: depot stands up (case 2 ran). Read moniker from rbrr.env.

Steps:
1. Read moniker from rbrr.env. If blank, Verdict::Fail with "case 2 (stand-up) did not run or rbrr.env is missing the moniker".
2. Mantle: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE, &[], &[], dir, "mantle")`. Fail-fast.
3. Read `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL` from mantle's BURV via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&mantle, RBTDRP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL)`. Tee to `dir.join("governor-sa-email.txt")` (diagnostic only — no assertion).
4. Verify governor RBRA was deposited: `assert RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` exists. Use the same path-derivation pattern the existing case 3 uses (read RBRR_SECRETS_DIR from rbrr.env via `rbtdrp_read_env_value`, join `<role>/rbra.env`). Define a small helper `rbtdrp_canonical_rbra(root, role) -> PathBuf` if useful.

   Actually — IMPORTANT NUANCE — `rbgp_governor_mantle` writes RBRA directly to `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical), not to assay. This is because mantle is a Payor-owned operation that drops the operator's governor key into place. Verify by reading `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` mantle function. If your reading confirms this, your assertion is `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` exists post-mantle. If not, adjust accordingly.

5. Invest retriever: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER, &[RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER], &[], dir, "invest-retriever")`. Fail-fast.
6. Verify assay has retriever's RBRA: `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` exists.
7. Copy assay → canonical retriever: `std::fs::copy(assay, retriever_canonical)`. Map error to Verdict::Fail.
8. Access-probe retriever: `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE, "retriever", &[])`. Tee result. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
9. Invest director: same pattern with `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR` + `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR`.
10. Copy assay → canonical director.
11. Access-probe director.
12. Divest director: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR, &[RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR], &[], dir, "divest-director")`. Fail-fast.
13. Verify director canonical RBRA was removed: `!RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE.exists()`. (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA contract.)
14. Divest retriever: same pattern.
15. Verify retriever canonical RBRA removed.
16. Cleanup: `std::fs::remove_file(assay)` if exists. Ignore errors (best-effort).
17. Verdict::Pass.

### Case 4 (NEW): `rbtdrp_depot_tear_down`
Pre-condition: depot exists (cases 2-3 ran).

Steps:
1. Read moniker from rbrr.env. If blank, Verdict::Fail.
2. Compose project_id for diagnostic.
3. Unmake: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_UNMAKE, &[], &[(RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY, RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP)], dir, "unmake")`. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
4. List-after: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST, &[], &[], dir, "list-after")`.
5. Verify the depot is absent or in DELETE_REQUESTED state via fact-file read (NOT stdout-grep): read `<moniker>.depot` from list-after's BURV. If file absent → depot is gone → Pass. If file present → read content; must be `DELETE_REQUESTED` (use `RBTDRP_DELETE_REQUESTED` const). Anything else → Fail with diagnostic.
6. Verdict::Pass.

## Constraints

- Match the existing error idiom: `match ... { Ok(r) => r, Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(...)) }`.
- Each case is `fn rbtdrp_<name>(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict { rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| rbtdrp_<name>_impl(ctx, dir)) }` with a `_impl` companion taking `&mut rbtdri_Context`.
- Use `rbtdrp_invoke_logged` for all tabtarget invocations to get stdout/stderr tee'd.
- Add new module-level consts where needed (family stem, identities, fact-ext for depot-project, fact-name for governor SA email is already there). Place new consts adjacent to existing related ones.
- Use `RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project` value `"depot-project"` — define `RBTDRP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT: &str = "depot-project"` mirror const.
- Do NOT touch the existing case 1 (`rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation`).
- Do NOT touch the existing helpers (they're correct as-is).

## Section registry

The new `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` should be:
```rust
pub static RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE: &[rbtdre_Section] = &[
    rbtdre_Section {
        name: "pristine-lifecycle-gate",
        cases: &[case!(rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation)],
    },
    rbtdre_Section {
        name: "pristine-lifecycle-arc",
        cases: &[
            case!(rbtdrp_depot_stand_up),
            case!(rbtdrp_sa_cycle),
            case!(rbtdrp_depot_tear_down),
        ],
    },
];
```

## Imports needed

The new pristine.rs will import (replacing the current import block from rbtdrm_manifest):
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_UNMAKE`
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE`
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR`
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_RETRIEVER`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR`
- `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE`

Drop: `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_LIST_SAS`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_FORFEIT`.

Also need `rbtdri_invoke_imprint` import from `crate::rbtdri_invocation` if not already imported.

## Your report

Provide:
1. **Confirmation of file reads** — one line per file, noting any deviation from my assumed shape (especially: whether `rbgp_governor_mantle` writes RBRA to canonical or assay; whether `rbtdrc_access_probe_role` is `pub(crate)` or private; what API exists in `rbtdri_invocation.rs` for fact-file reading).

2. **Full Rust source for the three case functions** plus their `_impl` companions, plus any new module-level consts, plus any small helper (e.g., `rbtdrp_canonical_rbra`) you introduce. Wrap in a single ```rust code block. The code should be drop-in copy-pasteable into the file (the main thread will splice it in).

3. **The new import block** for the `use crate::rbtdrm_manifest::{...};` line.

4. **The new `RBTDRP_SECTIONS_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE` static** as it should appear.

5. **A short verification list** of items the main thread should sanity-check (e.g., "verify mantle writes to canonical RBRA path, not assay" if you found something surprising).

Keep your report under 1200 words excluding the code block.

Note: helpers `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes`, `rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker`, `rbtdrp_compose_project_id`, `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker`, `rbtdrp_invoke_logged`, `rbtdrp_replace_env_fields`, `rbtdrp_git_add_and_commit` are all already in place. Static const `RBTDRP_DELETE_REQUESTED = "DELETE_REQUESTED"`. Const `RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY` / `RBTDRP_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP` for unmake env.

2. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdri_invocation.rs` — `rbtdri_invoke_global`, `rbtdri_invoke_imprint`, `rbtdri_read_burv_fact`, `rbtdri_Context`, `rbtdri_InvokeResult`. Note: `result.burv_output` plus the `RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR` constant locate per-invocation fact files.

3. `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` (lines around 2847) — see `rbtdrc_access_probe_role(ctx, role, dir)` for the access-probe pattern. You'll re-use the same shape (or call it directly if reachable; check whether the function is `pub(crate)` — if private, replicate the call inline).

4. `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh` (lines 528-590) — `rbgg_invest_retriever` and `rbgg_invest_director` take an identity arg (param1 channel), write the new SA's RBRA file to `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`. Confirm via reading these files.

6. `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — `RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"` (the fact-extension for the project_id fact emitted by `rbgp_depot_list`).

## Design decisions (already made — do not redesign)

- **Section name:** `pristine-lifecycle-arc`
- **Family stem for moniker autodetect:** `pristl` (constant `RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC`, value `"pristl"`)
- **Static identities:** retriever `pristl-ret`, director `pristl-dir` (consts `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER`, `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR`)
- **Cross-case state mechanism:** `rbrr.env` (moniker survives across cases); each case re-derives project_id via `rbtdrp_compose_project_id(root, moniker)` where moniker is read from rbrr.env via `rbtdrp_read_env_value(&rbrr, RBTDRP_FIELD_RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`.
- **No stdout-grep assertions.** Use `Path::exists()` on canonical RBRA paths after invest/probe/divest, and a fact-content read for project_id post-levy. Access-probe success IS the SA-presence assertion.
- **Invest writes RBRA to assay; test copies assay → role canonical.** Static `std::fs::copy` (not rename — preserve assay for diagnostic post-mortem until divest).
- **No separate forfeit governor step.** Tear-down's unmake absorbs governor cleanup per BBAAN's 404-tolerant ordering.
- **Read project_id from fact file** (not stdout, not compose). After `rbgp_depot_list`, read `<moniker>.depot-project` from the BURV output dir via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact_multi` (or whatever the multi-fact reader is named — check rbtdri_invocation.rs for the API). If only single-fact reader exists, read the file directly using `result.burv_output.join(RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR).join(format!("{}.{}", moniker, RBTDRP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT))`.

## The three cases

### Case 2 (NEW): `rbtdrp_depot_stand_up`
Pre-condition: rbrr.env may be marshal-zero blank.

Steps:
1. `rbtdrp_install_throwaway_prefixes(&root)` (idempotent self-commit).
2. List-pre via `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST, &[], &[], dir, "list-pre")`. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
3. Pick moniker via `rbtdrp_pick_next_moniker(&list_pre, RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC)`.
4. Install moniker via `rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker(&root, &moniker)` (self-commit).
5. Levy via `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY, &[], &[], dir, "levy")`. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
6. Re-list to refresh facts: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LIST, &[], &[], dir, "list-present")`.
7. Read project_id from `<moniker>.depot-project` fact file via direct file read (compose path from `list_present.burv_output`). Tee to `dir.join("project-id.txt")`.
8. Sanity-cross-check: the composed project_id (`rbtdrp_compose_project_id`) must equal the fact-read project_id. If not, Verdict::Fail with diagnostic showing both. (This catches RBDC kindle drift from payor's actual creation.)
9. Verdict::Pass.

### Case 3 (NEW): `rbtdrp_sa_cycle`
Pre-condition: depot stands up (case 2 ran). Read moniker from rbrr.env.

Steps:
1. Read moniker from rbrr.env. If blank, Verdict::Fail with "case 2 (stand-up) did not run or rbrr.env is missing the moniker".
2. Mantle: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE, &[], &[], dir, "mantle")`. Fail-fast.
3. Read `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL` from mantle's BURV via `rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&mantle, RBTDRP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL)`. Tee to `dir.join("governor-sa-email.txt")` (diagnostic only — no assertion).
4. Verify governor RBRA was deposited: `assert RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` exists. Use the same path-derivation pattern the existing case 3 uses (read RBRR_SECRETS_DIR from rbrr.env via `rbtdrp_read_env_value`, join `<role>/rbra.env`). Define a small helper `rbtdrp_canonical_rbra(root, role) -> PathBuf` if useful.

   Actually — IMPORTANT NUANCE — `rbgp_governor_mantle` writes RBRA directly to `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` (canonical), not to assay. This is because mantle is a Payor-owned operation that drops the operator's governor key into place. Verify by reading `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` mantle function. If your reading confirms this, your assertion is `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE` exists post-mantle. If not, adjust accordingly.

5. Invest retriever: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER, &[RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER], &[], dir, "invest-retriever")`. Fail-fast.
6. Verify assay has retriever's RBRA: `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` exists.
7. Copy assay → canonical retriever: `std::fs::copy(assay, retriever_canonical)`. Map error to Verdict::Fail.
8. Access-probe retriever: `rbtdri_invoke_imprint(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ACCESS_PROBE, "retriever", &[])`. Tee result. Fail-fast on non-zero exit.
9. Invest director: same pattern with `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR` + `RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR`.
10. Copy assay → canonical director.
11. Access-probe director.
12. Divest director: `rbtdrp_invoke_logged(ctx, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR, &[RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR], &[], dir, "divest-director")`. Fail-fast.
13. Verify director canonical RBRA was removed: `!RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE.exists()`. (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA contract.)
14. Divest retriever: same pattern.
15. Verify retriever canonical RBRA removed.
16. Cleanup: `std::fs::remove_file(assay)` if exists. Ignore errors (best-effort).
17. Verdict::Pass.

### Case 4 (NEW): `rbtdrp_depot_tear_down`
Pre-condition: depot exists (cases 2-3 ran).

Steps:
1. Read moniker from rbrr.env. If blank, Verdict::Fail.
2. Compose project_id for diagnostic.

**Phase 2 — Integration:** Spliced Agent P's source into `rbtdrp_pristine.rs` via 7 edits. Net: +227 lines (392 ins / 165 del). File now 1107 lines.

**Phase 3 — Verification:**
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh`: green
- `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh`: 58/58 pass

Orthogonal to this pace's scope.

**Phase 4 — Review:** No scope creep, no drift.
- Manifest diff matches brief exactly; no incidental edits to other consts.
- Pristine.rs structure clean: helpers preserved untouched, case 1 untouched, three new cases match docket rev [6] design (depot stand-up → SA cycle → tear-down).
- Stdout-grep retirement complete: zero `stdout.contains` references in the file.
- Old family stem consts (`FAMILY_STEM_LIFECYCLE`, `FAMILY_STEM_GOVERNOR`) cleanly removed; new `FAMILY_STEM_ARC` plus identity consts in place.
- Section registry: §2 renamed to `pristine-lifecycle-arc`, three cases registered.
- Cross-case state flows through rbrr.env (moniker survives); each case re-derives project_id via `rbtdrp_compose_project_id`.

**Out-of-band note:** The pristine fixture itself is service-tier (requires GCP credentials + marshal-zero state). Build + manifest verification proves structural correctness. Full end-to-end verification awaits actual `rbw-tP` invocation against live cloud — that's the heat's terminal milestone, orthogonal to this pace's mechanical scope.

params.intent: Rebuild pristine-lifecycle fixture as fused arc section per BBAAK docket rev [6]. §2 'pristine-lifecycle-simple' (cases depot-lifecycle, governor-lifecycle — each levying its own throwaway depot, four per fixture run total) replaced by §2 'pristine-lifecycle-arc' — three cases sharing one throwaway depot: (1) rbtdrp_depot_stand_up — install throwaway prefixes, pick moniker in pristl family, install moniker, levy, re-list, read project_id from <moniker>.depot-project fact, sanity-cross-check against RBDC compose derivation; (2) rbtdrp_sa_cycle — pre-condition check on RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER, mantle governor (locate governor-<timestamp>.rbra in BURV output, copy to canonical), invest retriever pristl-ret + copy assay to retriever canonical + access-probe, invest director pristl-dir + copy to director canonical + access-probe, divest director (verify canonical RBRA absent per BBAAN divest-deletes-production-RBRA contract), divest retriever (same), best-effort assay cleanup; (3) rbtdrp_depot_tear_down — read moniker, compose project_id for diagnostic, unmake (BURE_CONFIRM=skip), re-list, verify depot fact absent or DELETE_REQUESTED via fact-content read. Manifest changes: removed dead consts RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_LIST_SAS (rbw-aL) and RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_FORFEIT (rbw-aF) — neither tabtarget exists post-BBAAN; added RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_RETRIEVER (rbw-arI), RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_INVEST_DIRECTOR (rbw-adI), RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_RETRIEVER (rbw-arD), RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_DIVEST_DIRECTOR (rbw-adD); RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PRISTINE_LIFECYCLE required-colophons array expanded from 6 to 9 (DEPOT_LEVY/LIST/UNMAKE + GOV_MANTLE + 4 invest/divest + ACCESS_PROBE). Pristine.rs changes: imports updated (drop GOV_LIST_SAS/GOV_FORFEIT, add 4 invest/divest + ACCESS_PROBE; add rbtdri_invoke_imprint and RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR); new helper rbtdrp_canonical_rbra(root, role) reading RBRR_SECRETS_DIR from rbrr.env; new module-level consts RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_ARC='pristl', RBTDRP_IDENTITY_RETRIEVER='pristl-ret', RBTDRP_IDENTITY_DIRECTOR='pristl-dir', RBTDRP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT='depot-project'; old consts RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_LIFECYCLE='pristq' and RBTDRP_FAMILY_STEM_GOVERNOR='pristg' retired. All stdout-substring assertions retired — observations now via Path::exists() on canonical RBRA paths and fact-content reads from BURV output dirs (no list_present.stdout.contains, no list_after.stdout.contains). Cross-case state flows through rbrr.env (RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER persisted by case 1 install, re-read by cases 2 and 3); project_id re-derived per case via rbtdrp_compose_project_id. Cost reduction: one throwaway depot per fixture run (down from four under the prior BBAAJ+BBAAK design). Phased via two parallel sonnet agents (Agent M for manifest in-place, Agent P for pristine.rs as text deliverable) plus main-thread integration; agents file-disjoint at delivery time. Surfaced design correction during agent dispatch: rbgp_governor_mantle writes RBRA to BURV output as governor-<timestamp>.rbra (not directly to canonical), so SA cycle case globs the file and copies to canonical the same way invest paths do — discovered by Agent P's read of rbgp_Payor.sh, applied without rework. Pristine fixture itself is service-tier — requires GCP credentials and marshal-zero state to exercise live; structural correctness verified via build + manifest, full live-cycle verification awaits rbw-tP invocation in a future heat milestone.
